expectations.text: Each issue lists specific files or directories it will modify (not vague references like 'the API layer')
expectations.evidence: Every issue has a 'Files to modify' section listing specific file paths like '/tmp/widgetin/src/server/api/routes/widget.ts'.

expectations.text: Output includes a parallel lanes or concurrency view showing which issues can run simultaneously
expectations.evidence: No parallel lanes or concurrency view. Only a sequential 'Suggested Implementation Order' (1-13) is provided.

expectations.text: No two issues that are simultaneously unblocked (same lane position, no dependency between them) share the same files
expectations.evidence: 11 of 13 issues are simultaneously unblocked (only #10 depends on #9). Issues 3, 4, 5, 8, 11, 12, 13 all independently touch widget.ts + embed-renderer.ts + page.tsx. Running any two of these in parallel would cause merge conflicts on all three shared files.

expectations.text: Issues that touch shared files (e.g., widgetConfigSchema, embed-renderer.ts) are chained via dependencies rather than left independent
expectations.evidence: Only one dependency exists (#10 depends on #9). Issues 3, 4, 5, 8, 11, 12, 13 all touch the same files (widget.ts, embed-renderer.ts, page.tsx) but have no dependencies between them.

expectations.text: File paths referenced in the breakdown correspond to actual files in the widgetin codebase
expectations.evidence: File paths match real files in the repo. Uses absolute paths (/tmp/widgetin/...) rather than relative, but they're correct.

expectations.text: Issues are vertical slices (end-to-end), not horizontal layers (e.g., not 'all schema changes' as one issue)
expectations.evidence: Each issue is a complete vertical slice. E.g., Issue 3 'Reviewer Name Format' touches schema (widget.ts), renderer (embed-renderer.ts), editor UI (page.tsx), DB defaults (widgetin.ts), and tests. Issue 5 'Granular Review Card Style Controls' similarly spans all layers.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline performs as expected — good vertical slices with file paths, but zero conflict awareness.
